feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: sac excel addin comments
entry.content:
Hello gurus, would like to clarify the support of comments functionality when using the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Add-in for Excel in combination with SAP Datasphere analytical models.

Could you please confirm are comments (cell/data point comments) supported in the SAC Excel Add-in when the data source is based on SAP Datasphere?

entry.title: SAP Datasphere - Connections menu is not visible on the Left hand side menubar
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We are not able to see the Connections menu in the left hand side menu bar even after logging in as an administrator. 

attachaed are two images 

  1. HPE-image - Is our datasphere screen showing "Connections" menu is not visible
  2. other-customer-image - is screenshot from other customer's datasphere where connections menu is visible

Regards,

Vaibhav

entry.title: Datasphere: Import objects with data included (LT) did not work properly.
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We are experiencing an issue while transporting objects between our SAP Datasphere tenants.

Source Tenant (Production)
Target Tenant (Quality)

Our objective is to transport Local Tables including their data from the Production tenant to the Quality tenant.

The process we followed is:

  1. Create an Export Job in the Production tenant including the required Local Tables.
  2. Enable the "Data Included" option during the export process.
  3. Execute the export and successfully generate the transport package.
  4. Import the package into the Quality tenant.

The issue is that, although the "Data Included" option is selected during the export, the Local Table definitions are imported correctly but the table data is not transferred to the ta

entry.title: How to send data extracts and replications from SAP S4 to MS Fabrics
entry.content:
Hello Mates,

Good day!

How to integrate and send data extracts and replications from SAP S4 HANA Private Cloud(RISE) to MS Fabrics via Standalone SAP SLT DMIS2020? Is this considered an strategic solution to use this option quickly?

Only 2 option comes in to my mind as optimal one is SAP datasphere and another is Standalone SAP SLT.

Please require your kind inputs. Appreciate it!

 

Thanks and Regards,

Rajesh PS

entry.title: How do I grant a Datasphere Open SQL Schema user read access to Datasphere-native objects?
entry.content:
Problem

We would like to create a database user in a Datasphere space that has read access to all local tables within that space, without having to manually expose each table via a view individually.

The standard approach requires exposing every object explicitly before it becomes visible to the Open SQL Schema user (<space>#<user>). In our case, the space contains a large number of local tables, and maintaining individual exposure per table is not practical, especially as new tables are added continuously.

What we are looking for is a way to grant blanket read access to all local tables in a space for a database user, similar to a GRANT SELECT ON SCHEMA <schema> TO <user> in SAP HANA, without the per-object exposure step in Datasphere.

Questions

  1. Is there a supported way to grant schema-level or space-level read access to all local tables for an Open SQL Schema database user, without exposing each object individually?

  2. If not, is there a supported workaround to automate bulk exposure?

Any input or experience with this is appreciated.

entry.title: Duplicate Key Predicate Error in Analytic Model in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I am currently working on converting the Analytical Datasets (now deprecated) to Analytic Model using Fact semantic usage as is recommended. While doing so, I encountered an error in the Analytic Model where I was unable to see the data in the Data Preview and is showed an error saying "Duplicate key predicate:(PRIMARY KEY DIMENSIONS = value)" i.e. showing only primary key dimensions and a particular value. I can see data in the Data Preview in the Fact View that is used as the base source of the Analytical Model. I had to remove the Primary Key of these dimensions and only then could I see the data. And then there was another error saying: "Duplicate key predicate:(null). Is there any resolution for this?


Please let me know if I can provide any more information.


Thank you!

entry.title: Data ingestion issue for standard SAP ODP- Extractor
entry.content:
We are facing a data ingestion issue with the standard SAP ODP Extractor using Replication Flow for the delta load mechanism.

When delta capture is enabled, the replication flow fails with an error indicating that the primary key is not configured.

Could you please advise on the recommended approach for extracting delta data from standard SAP ODP Extractors? Specifically, we would like to understand:

  • The prerequisites and configuration required for delta extraction.
  • Whether primary key configuration is mandatory for ODP-based delta loads.
  • The recommended process for enabling and consuming delta records through Replication Flows.
  • Any SAP notes, documentation, or best practices that should be followed for this scenario.

Your guidance on resolving this issue would be greatly appreciated

entry.title: Unable to use field in datasphere replication flow
entry.content:
Hi ,

we are using table for replication flow creation in datasphere .but we are unable to use one field MATID because datasphere is getting value unconverted from table instead of value converted .

i have attached screenshot for reference ,please check.

table name : /SCWM/ORDIM_C 

field name : MATID 

 

 

Regards,

sanjay

entry.title: Datasphere -HANA Tables/Views (in SYS Schema ?) that store mappings from source to target objects
entry.content:
HI,

Can you confirm what HANA Tables/Views (in SYS Schema ?)  store mappings from source ( e.g a projection) to a target (local table)

Thanks

Anthony

entry.title: SAC Running Total and Sorting – Is There a Workaround?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

we are facing an issue with Running Totals in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and are looking for possible workarounds or best practices.

Scenario

We have a story containing a measure that is displayed as a Running Total. As long as the table remains in its default dimension order, the cumulative values are calculated correctly.

However, as soon as a user changes the sort order of the table (e.g., Product A–Z, descending, by measure value, etc.), the Running Total no longer reflects the displayed order. The cumulative values appear incorrect from the user's perspective.

Senario from sap for me: 

 

 

Findings So Far

According to SAP KBA 3294430, this behavior is by design.

In short, the Running Total calculation is based on the underlying hierarchy or dimension order in the model rather than the sort order currently displayed in the SAC table. As a result, the displayed Running Total can become misleading once users apply their own sorting.

Attempted Workaround

As an alternative, we tried to simulate a Running Total using table formulas similar to Excel formulas, for example: =D3+B4 or =$D3+$B$4

Initially, this works as expected. However, once the user changes the table sorting, the formulas produce #REF! errors because the cell references are no longer valid.

Question

Has anyone found a reliable workaround to calculate a Running Total that follows the current sort order displayed in the SAC table?

Alternatively, is there a way to implement such a calculation using SAC table formulas, calculated measures, scripting, or another approach that remains stable even when users change the sorting?

Any ideas, recommendations, or experiences would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP C4C to DSP connection is established but exposed tables are not showing in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

Need your help we have established the connection between Cloud for Customer C4C to Datasphere . 

In Datasphere I want to fetch the data from one of the remote source tables OpportunityHeader  and it is exposed via ODATA link 

In this path when I tried to verify the table it is not showing in path. 

I can see other table like Opportunity collection but Header table is not showing.

Could you please suggest what is missing or how to extract the exposed tables into Datasphere  

Note:  These tables are standard table but not customized.

Awaiting your feedback.

entry.title: Best practices on self service roles
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have an issue with security roles. 
Introduction: With my team we are starting a self-service project where users will log into sap analytics cloud and can create their own stories.
Context: we have the "scoped user" role assig to these users, with this role they can see data from models in the "QA" and "PRD" spaces,  because they need access to them to be able to see any data on the stories developed by the data engineers.
issue: With this self-service project, this role that I mentioned before (scoped user) lets them see and use all the models in the "QA" and "PRD" spaces in sap analytics cloud, and we only want them to be able to access the models on their own space (EG: If the user is from human resources area, they will only have access to the Human Resources space in datasphere for self service).

Is the issue explained clearly enough?

have a nice week.

entry.title: Triggering events from SAP Datasphere Task Chains in HDLFS Spaces
entry.content:
We are looking for a way to trigger events from SAP Datasphere that can be consumed by downstream systems for scheduling and error-handling purposes.

Our requirement is to notify an enterprise scheduling tool when a Datasphere task chain completes, either successfully or with an error. Our preferred approach is to invoke an API endpoint at the end of the task chain, thereby avoiding the need to continuously poll Datasphere for job status.

To achieve this, we explored using API Tasks within Task Chains, as they can invoke external APIs through a Generic HTTP Connection.

However, we found that our Datasphere space is of type HDLFS, and Generic HTTP Connections are not supported in HDLFS spaces.

Documentation references:

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/c8a54ee704e94e15926551293243fd1d/9a8489ed7443436197fbd8b8ffba61ab.html

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/be5967d099974c69b77f4549425ca4c0/b79b865e64794c7abcd12a54f2f73c8c.html

Has anyone faced a similar requirement?

We would appreciate any guidance on the following:

  • Are there alternative mechanisms in SAP Datasphere to trigger external events or invoke external APIs when a task chain completes?

  • Has anyone integrated Datasphere task chains with an external scheduler or orchestration tool using an event-driven approach?

  • What approaches have you used to avoid polling for task chain status?

  • Are there any recommended best practices for this type of integration in HDLFS spaces?

Any insights or examples would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: SAC Writeback to Datasphere
entry.content:
We are testing the ability for SAP Analytics Cloud to write data to SAP Datasphere. We have encountered an error when trying to do this. 

Step 1 - Created an OAuth client in SAC

Step 2 - Configured Datasphere with connection details, OAuth 2.0 authentication, Grant Type of Client Credentials, provided OAuth Token Endpoint, OAuth Response Type = token, OAuth Token Request Content Type: URL encoded.

Step 3 - Provided Client ID and Secret

Step 4 - Entered Name and Description

Step 5 - Received Connection Created Successfully message. Connection is listed under Datasphere Connections screen.

Step 6 - Click Validate the Connection. ERROR - Connection couldn't be established: Data flows can't be used because of errors in the connection; Replication flows are not supported; Remote tables are disabled. Data Flows: Cause: [CloudDataIntegration] Cannot connect to service because Failed to fetch OAuth2 token. Token endpoint response HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized["error": "invalid_client","error_description":"Unauthorized grant type]

entry.title: Integration Scenarios for Non-SAP Systems with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
We currently have the below non-SAP systems using data transfer mechanisms as outlined below:

MIMS → Data Services → HANA DB 
ICIMS → API/SFTP (via CPI) → SAP BW 
DS_MasterEngine_SLA (API) → HANA DB 
OSIsoft (AVEVA) → HANA DB 

As part of the BW/4HANA transformation initiative, we are planning to integrate these data sources directly with SAP Datasphere by eliminating the existing Data Services and HANA DB ( layers wherever possible.

Could you please help us evaluate and suggest suitable integration scenarios/approaches for the above systems with SAP Datasphere?


Please also share if there are any reference architectures or implementation documents available for similar integrations.

entry.title: AM error in RL Space: Table-like entities with parameters are not supported for conversion to SQL
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
 
I am facing an issue in SAP Datasphere Analytical Model when consuming a parameterized object from another space.
 
Scenario:
I have created a view for Business Partner Relationship data. The view contains an input parameter for key-date based validity filtering.
 
The input parameter is:
VERTRIEBSPARTNER_GULTIGKEITSDATUM
The datatype of the input parameter and the date fields is the same.
What is working:
I created a Dimension with Hierarchy based on this parameterized view.
The Dimension and Hierarchy are working correctly. Data preview works successfully with the input parameter.

 

I also tested the same dataset and the same fact view in the same space by creating a new Analytical Model. In that case, the Analytical Model works correctly and I do not get any error.

 

What is not working:
The issue occurs only when the Analytical Model is created in the Reporting Space.

 

In the Reporting Space, when I consume the same parameterized object / Dimension / Hierarchy in an Analytical Model, I get the following error:
"Table-like entities with parameters are not supported for conversion to SQL"
 
Can anyone confirm whether SAP Datasphere Analytical Model supports direct consumption of parameterized views / dimensions / hierarchies from another space?
Has anyone faced a similar issue with this error?
Any suggestion or confirmation would be helpful.

 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Spaces using HDLF -- how many can be created and how many with write access
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need some clarity on Datasphere Spaces using HDLF:

1. For a BDC Tenant, how many Datasphere Spaces using HDLF can be created?

2. Out of the created Datasphere Spaces using HDLF, how many have ability to create localtables and do inserts on those localtables?

Thanks.

Regards,

Mel Calucin

@jeet_kapase 

@FernandaFroelich

entry.title: Datasphere data flow null value in target table
entry.content:
I created a data flow to improve data persistence. It goes from Graphical View -> Data Flow (scheduled to run every 20mins) -> Local Table -> Analytic Model.

This works fine but somehow there is a column that does not receive any values (just NULLs). It's a date column. It is mapped correctly. It is labeled as date on both sides. When I data preview in the data flow, the source shows the values for that column but the target just shows NULLs.

 

Any ideas what could be wrong or where to fix it?

entry.title: SAP Datasphere: How to create or reuse Replication Flows and Local table with 2 Source System ?
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

A client with multiple companies currently has SAP S4HANA PRD and SAP Datasphere PRD in production, both containing data from Company 1.

They also have S4HANA QAS1 and SAP Datasphere QAS1 in quality assurance. Now, SAP Datasphere QAS1 needs to extract data from a new S4HANA QAS2 repository belonging to Company 2.

Should I create new replication flows and local tables, or use the existing local tables and only create the replication flow? 

My main concern is the transportation of local tables and replication flows that already exist in production.

 

Regards.

entry.title: I would like to expose 3 specific views from schema SYS in HANA DB to Datasphere for reporting, how?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm building a "where-used" / impact-analysis report in SAP Datasphere. The idea: for any given connection, list the objects that depend on it (remote tables, views, analytic models), so we can see what would be affected before changing, renaming or removing a connection.

The dependency information I need lives in three HANA system views in the SYS schema:

  • SYS.VIRTUAL_TABLES
  • SYS.OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES
  • SYS.REMOTE_SOURCES

What I'm trying to do: consume these three SYS views inside Data Builder (a Graphical/SQL View) so I can join them and build the reporting view. I only need to read metadata — no business data at all.

What I've already done and observed:

  • Created a SAP HANA connection back to our own tenant database, authenticated with a Database Analysis user.
  • That user can read all three SYS views fine in the Database Explorer (SELECT returns rows).
  • It cannot read any space business data (SELECT on a space table → [258] insufficient privilege) — which is exactly the least-privilege scope I want.
  • But in Data Builder → Import Remote Tables, the SYS schema is not listed (user schemas and DWC_GLOBAL appear, SYS does not), so I can't select these views.
  • Workaround that works: create pass-through views (SELECT * FROM SYS.<view>) in a normal user schema and import those. It works — but I'd rather not create helper views in the database if there's a supported, native way.

My questions:

  1. Is there a supported way to expose/import these three SYS views into Data Builder (via a connection or otherwise) without creating helper views in the database? For example: a way to manually specify a system-schema object during import, a setting to surface system schemas on a connection, or a recommended pattern for consuming SYS catalog metadata in Datasphere.
  2. For a permanent setup, what's the recommended stable, read-only technical user with catalog read access (CATALOG READ) — e.g. a global Database User Group user — given the Database Analysis user is meant to be temporary?

Thanks in advance for any pointers!
